executive_summary: Overall Fairness Risk: CRITICAL (score: 0.87). 8 of 10 fairness metrics failed. 16 findings detected (2 critical, 8 high, 1 low, 5 medium).

findings.description: Group 'Non-binary' in 'gender' has only 12 samples. Fairness metrics for this subgroup may be unstable.
findings.recommendation: Interpret subgroup fairness results with caution. Collect more representative data for under-sampled groups.

findings.description: Group 'Non-binary' in 'gender' is underrepresented: 12 samples (24.5% of the largest group).
findings.recommendation: Consider collecting more data for 'Non-binary' or applying rebalancing techniques. Under-representation can reduce model quality and fairness for minority groups.

findings.description: Positive outcome rates differ by 57.1% across 'gender' groups.
findings.recommendation: Large outcome disparities may reflect historical discrimination or biased labels. Review whether the target variable is a fair proxy for the intended real-world outcome.

findings.description: Demographic parity failed for 'gender'. Positive outcome rate gap is 57.1%.
findings.recommendation: Review decision thresholds, label generation, and subgroup representation to reduce outcome disparities.

findings.description: Disparate impact failed for 'gender'. Impact ratio is 0.30, below the 0.80 threshold.
findings.recommendation: Investigate whether one group is receiving substantially fewer positive outcomes. Review selection criteria, thresholds, and historical training data.

findings.description: Equal opportunity failed for 'gender'. True positive rate gap is 21.9%.
findings.recommendation: The model may correctly identify positive cases for some groups more than others. Review training balance, feature quality, and model thresholds.

findings.description: False negative rate parity failed for 'gender'. FNR gap is 21.9%.
findings.recommendation: Some groups may be denied positive outcomes more often despite being truly eligible. Review feature quality, thresholding, and subgroup performance.
